Title: Dual Regulation of Cuticle and Cell Wall Biosynthesis by BnaC9.MYB46 Confers Drought Tolerance in Brassica napus

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pCAMBIA1300 constitutive overexpression; pCAMBIA1301 RNAi suppression
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: 3-ketoacyl-CoA synthase (KCS), condensing enzyme of fatty acid elongase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed in rosette leaves; epidermis-specific promoter activity; induced by drought
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in rosette leaves; epidermis-specific promoter activity in transgenic Arabidopsis; induced by drought
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cuticular wax biosynthesis / very-long-chain fatty acid elongation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: VLCFA elongation supplying alkane-dominated wax branch
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream; committed/initial condensation step of elongation cycle
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Elongated very-long-chain 3-ketoacyl-CoA intermediates leading to C24+ wax precursors
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the first condensation step of very-long-chain fatty acid elongation, extending acyl-CoA chains to generate VLCFA precursors for wax biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for wax alkanes, secondary alcohols and ketones
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a rate-limiting/key effector step for VLCFA elongation in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Cuticular wax, especially C29 alkane; C29 secondary alcohol; C29 ketone
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Lipid polyester / wax (very-long-chain fatty acid-derived cuticular lipid)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hydrophobic barrier reducing non-stomatal water loss and improving drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cuticular wax accumulation, strengthens cuticle barrier, reduces water loss, and enhances drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaC2.KCS19 is directly activated by BnaC9.MYB46 through promoter binding and transactivation. 2. Overexpression increases total wax load and elevates C29 alkane, C29 secondary alcohol and C29 ketone, whereas RNAi reduces these wax classes and C24-C28 fatty acids. 3. Manipulation of BnaC2.KCS19 alters cuticular permeability, water retention, drought tolerance, and seed size/weight.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; RNAi suppression; qRT-PCR; promoter-GUS; subcellular localization; wax compositional profiling by GC/GC-MS; toluidine blue staining; chlorophyll leaching; water loss assay; drought survival assay; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; Y1H; dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No seed yield penalty detected under well-watered field conditions; slight reduction in germination rate under PEG in OE lines; increased seed size/weight observed
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among four KCS19 paralogs in B. napus, BnaC2.KCS19 showed strongest leaf-preferential expression. Expected chain-length specificity was not uniformly observed, possibly due to redundancy among KCS isoforms or downstream buffering.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising pathway target to increase drought tolerance via wax enhancement with limited yield penalty; useful downstream effector for stacking with BnaC9.MYB46 or alkane-branch genes such as CER1.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Target promoter assayed in transient dual-luciferase system; endogenous gene regulated in B. napus
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Aldehyde decarbonylase / CER1 wax alkane-forming protein family
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Activated by BnaC9.MYB46; discussed as wax-biosynthetic gene associated with alkane production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in BnaC9.MYB46 overexpression lines and reduced in RNAi lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought context relevant to wax-mediated tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Very-long-chain alkanes, especially C29 alkane-related output
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts very-long-chain aldehydes into alkanes in the wax alkane-forming pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Critical mediator of alkane production, but not explicitly rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Forms hydrophobic cuticle barrier that limits water loss and supports drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes alkane-rich wax accumulation downstream of BnaC9.MYB46
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaC9.MYB46 directly binds and activates the BnaA9.CER1-2 promoter. 2. The BnaC9.DEWAX1-BnaA9.CER1-2 module is highlighted as a critical mediator of wax phenotype and drought tolerance. 3. Increased CER1-2 expression is associated with alkane-rich cuticles in BnaC9.MYB46 OE plants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; Y1H; dual-luciferase transactivation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional importance supported in this paper mainly through regulatory evidence and prior literature cited for drought tolerance effects of BnaA9.CER1-2 overexpression.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target for boosting alkane-rich wax and drought resilience, especially in combination with repression of DEWAX1 or activation by BnaC9.MYB46.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in BnaC9.MYB46 OE and reduced in RNAi lines
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cuticular wax/cutin precursor biosynthesis via VLCFA elongation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for cuticular lipids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes cuticle lipid deposition as a direct downstream target of BnaC9.MYB46
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Transcript is positively regulated by BnaC9.MYB46. 2. BnaC9.MYB46 directly binds the promoter and activates transcription. 3. Gene is included in the coordinated cuticle lipid programme associated with drought tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; Y1H; dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No independent transgenic functional analysis in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in BnaC9.MYB46 OE and reduced in RNAi lines
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cuticular wax/cutin precursor biosynthesis via VLCFA elongation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for cuticular lipids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes cuticle lipid deposition as a direct downstream target of BnaC9.MYB46
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Transcript is positively regulated by BnaC9.MYB46. 2. BnaC9.MYB46 directly binds the promoter and activates transcription. 3. Gene forms part of the coordinated cuticle biosynthetic network.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; Y1H; dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No independent transgenic functional analysis in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in BnaC9.MYB46 OE and reduced in RNAi lines
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Acyl-CoA activation for cuticular lipid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates long-chain fatty acids to acyl-CoA thioesters for cutin/wax lipid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for cutin and wax biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Extracellular barrier formation reducing water loss
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely enhances cuticle lipid biosynthesis downstream of BnaC9.MYB46
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaC9.MYB46 directly targets and activates BnaC9.LACS3. 2. Expression increases in MYB46 OE lines and decreases in RNAi lines. 3. Included in the cuticle lipid biosynthetic network contributing to drought tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; Y1H; dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No independent genetic validation in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in BnaC9.MYB46 OE and reduced in RNAi lines
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Acyl-CoA activation for cuticular lipid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates long-chain fatty acids to acyl-CoA thioesters for cutin/wax lipid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for cutin and wax biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Extracellular barrier formation reducing water loss
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely enhances cuticle lipid biosynthesis downstream of BnaC9.MYB46
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaC9.MYB46 directly targets and activates BnaC1.LACS4. 2. Expression increases in MYB46 OE lines and decreases in RNAi lines. 3. Included in the coordinated cuticle programme associated with thicker cuticles.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; Y1H; dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No independent genetic validation in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in BnaC9.MYB46 OE and reduced in RNAi lines
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Acyl-CoA activation for cuticular lipid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates long-chain fatty acids to acyl-CoA thioesters for cutin/wax lipid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for cutin and wax biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Extracellular barrier formation reducing water loss
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely enhances cuticle lipid biosynthesis downstream of BnaC9.MYB46
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaC9.MYB46 directly targets and activates BnaA7.LACS9. 2. Expression increases in MYB46 OE lines and decreases in RNAi lines. 3. Included in the cuticle-related lipid metabolism module.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; Y1H; dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No independent genetic validation in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in BnaC9.MYB46 OE; modestly downregulated in RNAi line
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress context; secondary wall reinforcement
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Processed glucan chains facilitating cellulose microfibril assembly
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Endo-1,4-β-D-glucanase involved in cellulose biosynthesis and microfibril assembly
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for structural deposition of cellulose contributing to wall reinforcement
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enhances structural stability and vascular integrity under drought
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cellulose accumulation as part of BnaC9.MYB46-mediated secondary wall reinforcement
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaC9.MYB46 directly binds and activates the BnaC9.KOR1 promoter. 2. Expression is elevated in MYB46 OE plants together with increased cellulose deposition. 3. Gene contributes to drought-associated wall strengthening.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; histology; cell wall component quantification
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No independent genetic manipulation of KOR1 in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress context; secondary wall reinforcement
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: UDP-sugars involved in xylan-associated backbone synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Galacturonosyltransferase essential for xylan backbone formation in secondary cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports wall integrity and vascular stability under drought
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes hemicellulose deposition downstream of BnaC9.MYB46
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaC9.MYB46 directly binds and activates BnaC2.IRX8. 2. Expression increases in MYB46 overexpression plants concomitant with higher hemicellulose content. 3. Gene is part of the wall reinforcement programme improving drought resilience.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; histology; cell wall component quantification
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No independent genetic manipulation in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in BnaC9.MYB46 OE; modestly downregulated in RNAi line
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress context; secondary wall reinforcement
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 5-hydroxylated intermediates leading to syringyl lignin monomers
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates coniferaldehyde-related monolignol precursors in syringyl lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate precursor for syringyl lignin deposition
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Critical for syringyl lignin formation, but not explicitly rate-limiting here
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem and petiole vascular tissues; leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mechanical strength and vascular integrity under drought
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignin accumulation downstream of BnaC9.MYB46
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaC9.MYB46 directly binds and activates BnaC1.F5H. 2. MYB46 OE plants show increased lignification and higher lignin content. 3. The gene contributes to secondary wall reinforcement linked to drought adaptation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; histological lignin staining; cell wall component quantification
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion links F5H to S/G lignin ratio and vascular development based on prior Brassica literature.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pCAMBIA1300 constitutive overexpression; pCAMBIA1301 RNAi; native promoter-GUS (~1.5 kb) tested in Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor with R2 and R3 DNA-binding domains
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Broadly expressed with higher transcript levels in stem peels and developing siliques; enriched in leaf epidermal peels of high-wax cultivars; induced after 6 h PEG-simulated drought
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broad expression; promoter activity strong in leaves, especially epidermal cells, and active in stems/petioles; induced by PEG-simulated drought
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress; PEG-simulated drought; dehydration; field and chamber growth conditions
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cuticular wax biosynthesis, cutin-associated lipid metabolism, and secondary cell wall biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation of biosynthetic genes and transcriptional repression of a wax inhibitor
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: BnaA1.MYB106; BnaC9.LACS3; BnaC1.LACS4; BnaA7.LACS9; BnaC2.KCR1; BnaC5.KCR2; BnaC2.KCS19; BnaA9.CER1-2; BnaC9.DEWAX1; BnaC9.KOR1; BnaC2.IRX8; BnaC1.F5H
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates BnaA1.MYB106, BnaLACS3/4/9, BnaKCR1/KCR2, BnaC2.KCS19, BnaA9.CER1-2, BnaC9.KOR1, BnaC2.IRX8 and BnaC1.F5H; represses BnaC9.DEWAX1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central integrator/master regulator coordinating both cuticle fortification and secondary cell wall remodeling to improve drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: BnaA1.MYB106; BnaC9.LACS3; BnaC1.LACS4; BnaA7.LACS9; BnaC2.KCR1; BnaC5.KCR2; BnaC2.KCS19; BnaA9.CER1-2; BnaC9.DEWAX1; BnaC9.KOR1; BnaC2.IRX8; BnaC1.F5H (direct promoter targets)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Cuticular wax/cutin and secondary cell wall polymers (cellulose, hemicellulose, lignin)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Cuticular lipids and cell wall biopolymers
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf epidermal cuticle; stems and leaves; vascular tissues
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reduce non-stomatal water loss, preserve hydration, and stabilize vascular/mechanical integrity under drought
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances cuticle thickness, wax and cutin-like lipid deposition, cellulose/hemicellulose/lignin accumulation, and drought tolerance with little yield penalty
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaC9.MYB46 overexpression causes glaucous leaves, thicker cuticles, higher wax/cutin-like lipid content, reduced cuticular permeability, and better drought survival, whereas RNAi causes opposite cuticle phenotypes. 2. It directly activates multiple wax/cutin and secondary wall biosynthetic genes while repressing the wax inhibitor BnaC9.DEWAX1. 3. This dual regulation simultaneously reinforces the cuticle and secondary cell wall, establishing extracellular barriers that enhance drought resilience.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and RNAi in B. napus; qRT-PCR; promoter-GUS; subcellular localization; yeast transactivation assay; SEM/TEM; wax and cutin profiling; water loss/chlorophyll leaching/toluidine blue assays; drought survival/RWC assays; ROS/MDA/antioxidant enzyme assays; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; Y1H; dual-luciferase assays; histology and cell wall quantification
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No discernible differences in major agronomic and yield traits in MYB46 transgenic plants under tested conditions; mature field drought performance remains untested
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts beyond the epidermis, with promoter activity also in stems/petioles. RNAi effects on cell wall traits were mild, possibly due to functional compensation by constitutively expressed BnaMYB83.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value master regulator for breeding/engineering drought-resilient rapeseed via coordinated enhancement of cuticle and cell wall traits; suitable for gene stacking with downstream wax genes such as KCS19 and CER1 while monitoring context-dependent effects under field drought.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in BnaC9.MYB46 OE and elevated in RNAi lines
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of wax biosynthetic pathway genes; itself repressed by BnaC9.MYB46
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Suppresses BnaA9.CER1-2 expression according to this paper's discussion and cited prior work
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Critical negative regulator in the BnaC9.DEWAX1-BnaA9.CER1-2 wax module underpinning wax phenotype and drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator of wax accumulation; repression by BnaC9.MYB46 favors drought-protective wax deposition
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaC9.DEWAX1 expression is inversely correlated with BnaC9.MYB46 levels. 2. BnaC9.MYB46 directly binds and represses the DEWAX1 promoter. 3. The DEWAX1-CER1-2 module is identified as a critical mediator of alkane-rich wax accumulation and drought tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; Y1H; dual-luciferase repression assay
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional role as wax inhibitor is supported in this paper together with previous B. napus study cited by the authors.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for knockout or promoter attenuation to increase alkane wax deposition and drought tolerance, especially combined with CER1-2 activation.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in BnaC9.MYB46 OE and reduced in RNAi lines
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cuticle formation via wax and cutin biosynthesis regulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes to the cuticle regulatory network downstream of BnaC9.MYB46, but not established as decisive alone in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cuticle barrier formation and water retention
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes cuticle formation downstream of BnaC9.MYB46
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaA1.MYB106 is directly bound and transcriptionally activated by BnaC9.MYB46. 2. Its transcript level positively correlates with BnaC9.MYB46 abundance. 3. It is included in the coordinated transcriptional network enhancing cuticle development.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; Y1H; dual-luciferase assay
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No independent functional manipulation in this paper; role in wax/cutin regulation inferred also from Arabidopsis literature cited in Discussion.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary regulatory target for cuticle engineering, likely more useful in stacking strategies than as a standalone target.
